metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: Streamed response ended without content or tool calls

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the smoke test for AC #10: specify the exact command(s) to run (e.g., `pip install nava-rebar==0.7.1 && rebar --version`), the target environment, and the expected output that constitutes 'passes'.
findings.finding: The 'post-release smoke test' in both the Scope bullets and AC #10 ('The post-release smoke test passes against the published 0.7.1 artifact') is undefined: no command, no target, no pass/fail criterion. An executing agent cannot run or evaluate it without guessing.
findings.location: Scope / steps bullet 5; Acceptance Criteria AC #10
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC #10 with: 'Running `pip install nava-rebar==0.7.1 && rebar --version` in a clean virtualenv returns `rebar 0.7.1` (or equivalent CLI banner) with exit code 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or update) `docs/releasing.md` to remove/replace any manual GitHub Release creation and interactive MCP Registry login steps, reflecting that both are now fully automated by the `github_release` and `mcp_registry` workflow jobs.
findings.finding: The plan follows docs/releasing.md but does not include a step to verify (or update) that file to reflect the newly automated GitHub Release and MCP Registry flows. Since v0.7.0 required a manual GitHub Release ('manual one-off') and v0.7.1 is the first automated one, releasing.md may still describe manual steps for GitHub Release creation, leaving it stale after v0.7.1 ships.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a post-release step: 'Verify docs/releasing.md no longer references manual GitHub Release creation or interactive MCP Registry login; update if needed to match the automated v0.7.1 flow.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a recovery runbook (even a short bullet list) for each automated job failure: which jobs can be safely re-triggered, which require manual fallback, and what state to verify before retrying.
findings.finding: No operator recovery procedure is specified for partial-pipeline failure: if github_release or mcp_registry job fails after PyPI has already published, the plan gives no actionable steps (retry workflow? manual GitHub Release? manual registry push?), leaving the operator without a runbook for the first production run of new automation.
findings.location: Scope / steps — tag push pipeline; Verification note
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Failure recovery' subsection under Scope/steps: (1) If github_release fails: re-run that single job (it calls gh release create which is idempotent if the release doesn't yet exist, errors gracefully if it does). (2) If mcp_registry fails: re-run the job; OIDC tokens are per-job so a fresh token is issued on retry. (3) If PyPI publish fails and is retried after a partial upload, use `twine upload --skip-existing` or note that a re-upload of the same version will be rejected.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document (or configure) idempotent PyPI upload behavior — either confirm the workflow uses `--skip-existing` / `twine upload --skip-existing`, or note that a partial failure after PyPI acceptance requires manually triggering only the downstream jobs.
findings.finding: PyPI publish is non-idempotent: re-uploading the same version (0.7.1) after a partial workflow failure returns HTTP 400 'File already exists'. The plan does not acknowledge this constraint or specify how to recover (e.g., --skip-existing flag, or prohibition on re-running the full workflow).
findings.location: Scope / steps — 'publish (PyPI, OIDC)'; Acceptance Criteria — 'PyPI shows nava-rebar 0.7.1 live'
findings.suggested_fix: In the release.yml publish step, add `--skip-existing` to the twine/upload action so a retry after a partial failure proceeds past PyPI to the github_release and mcp_registry jobs without a 400 error.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `mcp_registry` workflow job (asserted as 'change 319' already landed) actually exists in `.github/workflows/release.yml` before tagging; as of the current codebase the file ends at the `github_release` job with no `mcp_registry` job present.
findings.finding: The plan's central premise — that 'the new `mcp_registry` job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319) auto-publishing server.json' is already landed — is directly contradicted by the codebase. `release.yml` contains exactly three jobs (`build`, `publish`, `github_release`) and ends at line 107; there is no `mcp_registry` job anywhere in the file or in any other workflow.
findings.location: ## What — 'the new mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319) auto-publishing server.json'
findings.suggested_fix: Add the missing `mcp_registry` job to `release.yml` (needs: github_release, trigger gate matching publish/github_release, permissions: id-token: write, steps to install mcp-publisher and publish server.json via OIDC) — or, if 'change 319' is not yet merged, make landing that change a prerequisite step in this plan before the tag push.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a plan step to update `docs/releasing.md` §4 (and the Accounts/prerequisites section) to replace the manual `mcp-publisher login github` device-flow instructions with the new OIDC-automated `mcp_registry` job description.
findings.finding: `docs/releasing.md` §4 (Update the MCP Registry) still describes the manual device/browser-auth flow (`mcp-publisher login github`) as the current procedure. The plan asserts that OIDC automation replaces this, but no update to `docs/releasing.md` is listed as a step — and the runbook will contradict the new automated behavior after the tag lands.
findings.location: Scope / steps — 'Tag v0.7.1 and push the tag -> release.yml runs'; docs/releasing.md §4 lines 119–139

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `mcp_registry` job referenced as 'change 319' is actually merged to main and present in `.github/workflows/release.yml` before pushing the v0.7.1 tag; if not, either land it first or update the plan/ACs to reflect that MCP Registry publishing remains manual for this release.
findings.finding: The plan's central new artifact — the `mcp_registry` job in `release.yml` that auto-publishes `server.json` via GitHub Actions OIDC (attributed to 'change 319') — does not exist in the repository. The actual `release.yml` ends after the `github_release` job (line 107) with no `mcp_registry` job anywhere in any workflow file. The plan's AC item 'The MCP Registry 0.7.1 entry was published by the mcp_registry OIDC job (no interactive login)' cannot be satisfied by the current codebase, and the `docs/releasing.md` §4 still describes the manual `mcp-publisher login github` (browser/device flow) as the publishing method.
findings.location: Plan section 'Scope / steps' / AC: 'The MCP Registry 0.7.1 entry was published by the mcp_registry OIDC job' / `.github/workflows/release.yml`
findings.suggested_fix: Check whether Gerrit change 319 is merged. If not yet merged, prepend a step: 'Merge change 319 (`mcp_registry` job) to main via Gerrit before tagging v0.7.1.' If the change cannot be landed in time, remove the mcp_registry AC and note MCP Registry as manual for this release, consistent with what `docs/releasing.md` currently describes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that 'change 319' (the `mcp_registry` job) is merged to main BEFORE pushing the v0.7.1 tag, or — if it has not landed — revise the plan to acknowledge that MCP Registry publishing for v0.7.1 will follow the existing manual `mcp-publisher login github` + `mcp-publisher publish` path documented in docs/releasing.md §4.
findings.finding: The plan's central premise — that 'the new mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319) auto-publishing server.json' is already landed and will fire on the v0.7.1 tag push — is contradicted by the codebase. The actual release.yml ends at the github_release job (line 107); no mcp_registry job exists anywhere in .github/workflows/. docs/releasing.md §4 still describes the manual mcp-publisher login github device-flow as the current method. Without the job in the workflow, pushing v0.7.1 will not trigger any automated MCP Registry publish, and the acceptance criterion 'published by the mcp_registry OIDC job (no interactive login)' will be unachievable as stated.
findings.location: Scope / steps — 'mcp_registry (auto MCP Registry, OIDC)' bullet; AC 'The MCP Registry 0.7.1 entry was published by the mcp_registry OIDC job'; .github/workflows/release.yml (no mcp_registry job present)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a prerequisite gate: verify `release.yml` contains a `mcp_registry` job with `id-token: write` before pushing the tag. If the job is absent, either (a) land change 319 first (making it a prerequisite step in the Scope), or (b) reframe v0.7.1 as exercising only the github_release automation and note MCP Registry remains manual for this release.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that 'change 319' (the `mcp_registry` job) is merged to main **before** tagging v0.7.1, or add a prerequisite gate to the plan making the tag contingent on that merge; the job does not exist in the current `release.yml`.
findings.finding: The plan treats the `mcp_registry` workflow job (attributed to 'change 319') as already landed and in production, but the actual `release.yml` contains only three jobs — `build`, `publish`, `github_release` — and ends at line 107. No `mcp_registry` job exists in the codebase. The plan's entire 'first true production run' premise for the MCP automation is predicated on code that is not yet present in the repo.
findings.location: Scope/steps section — 'mcp_registry (auto MCP Registry, OIDC; change 319)'; .github/workflows/release.yml (full file, lines 1-107)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit prerequisite: 'Verify `release.yml` contains the `mcp_registry` job (grep for `mcp_registry:` in `.github/workflows/release.yml`) before pushing the v0.7.1 tag. Do not tag until change 319 is merged and visible in main.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the unverified capability assertion that `mcp-publisher` supports a non-interactive GitHub Actions OIDC auth mode: confirm the exact CLI flag/subcommand (e.g. check the modelcontextprotocol/registry GitHub Actions docs or mcp-publisher release notes), verify any required pre-registration on the MCP Registry side, and document both in the plan before treating this as an established capability.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the MCP Registry accepts GitHub Actions OIDC tokens for non-interactive, automated publishing ('mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC)'). This is an unverified capability claim: every codebase reference to MCP Registry publishing — `docs/releasing.md` §4 and §Accounts/prerequisites — documents only the interactive `mcp-publisher login github` browser/device-flow OAuth path. No evidence exists in the repo that `mcp-publisher` supports a non-interactive OIDC mode callable from a GitHub Actions runner.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section — 'mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319) auto-publishing server.json'; docs/releasing.md lines 27-28, 133-134

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm change 319 (mcp_registry job) is merged to main and visible in `.github/workflows/release.yml` before pushing the v0.7.1 tag; if not, add its merge as an explicit prerequisite step in the plan.
findings.finding: The `mcp_registry` job that the plan describes as 'already-landed' (change 319) does not exist in `release.yml`. The workflow file ends at line 107 with only three jobs: `build`, `publish`, `github_release`. The plan's stated purpose — 'fully exercise the newly-landed release automation end-to-end' including 'auto-publishing server.json' via CI — is therefore impossible to achieve on a tag push with the current codebase.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit prerequisite gate: 'Verify `release.yml` contains a `mcp_registry` job (change 319 merged) before tagging. If not merged, land change 319 first, then cut the tag.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (against `mcp-publisher --help` or modelcontextprotocol.io/registry docs) that `mcp-publisher` supports a non-interactive GitHub Actions OIDC auth mode, and name the exact subcommand/flag in the plan and the workflow step.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the `mcp_registry` job publishes to the MCP Registry via 'GitHub Actions OIDC' without interactive login, but the only `mcp-publisher` auth subcommand evidenced anywhere in the codebase is `mcp-publisher login github` (browser/device-flow). There is no codebase evidence that `mcp-publisher` supports a non-interactive CI/OIDC mode (e.g., `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` or an environment-variable-based token). The named capability is UNVERIFIED.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For the `mcp_registry` workflow job (which holds `id-token: write`), specify how `mcp-publisher` is obtained on the runner — pin it to an exact version and verify its checksum or use a commit-SHA-pinned GitHub Action wrapper — to prevent a supply-chain attack on the OIDC-token-bearing runner.
findings.finding: The planned `mcp_registry` job will hold `id-token: write` (to authenticate against the MCP Registry via GitHub Actions OIDC) and execute the `mcp-publisher` external binary, but the plan specifies no supply-chain integrity contract for how that binary is obtained and verified on the privileged runner. A curl-fetched, unversioned, or unsigned binary executing on an `id-token: write` runner can exfiltrate the OIDC token or make unauthorized MCP Registry calls.
findings.location: Scope / steps — 'mcp_registry (auto MCP Registry, OIDC)' bullet; Verification note; AC 'The MCP Registry 0.7.1 entry was published by the mcp_registry OIDC job'
findings.suggested_fix: In the mcp_registry job step, either (a) install mcp-publisher via pip/pipx with an exact pinned version (`pipx install mcp-publisher==X.Y.Z`) and verify the hash, or (b) use a commit-SHA-pinned community Action (e.g., `uses: modelcontextprotocol/mcp-publisher-action@<full-sha>`) rather than a curl-fetched binary. The job should also declare `permissions: id-token: write` explicitly (and nothing else) so the scope is auditable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add (or confirm 'change 319' has landed) the `mcp_registry` job to `.github/workflows/release.yml` before pushing the v0.7.1 tag — it is absent from the current codebase and the plan's central AC depends on it.
findings.finding: The `mcp_registry` job — the plan's central new automation, attributed to 'change 319' — does not exist anywhere in `.github/workflows/release.yml` or any other workflow file. The actual release.yml has exactly three jobs: build, publish, github_release. The plan treats this job as already landed and instructs the executor to 'confirm success by inspecting the Actions run,' but there is nothing to run.
findings.location: Plan § 'What' / § 'Scope/steps' / Acceptance Criteria — 'The MCP Registry 0.7.1 entry was published by the mcp_registry OIDC job'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `mcp_registry` job to release.yml that: (a) needs: publish, (b) runs only on v* tag push, (c) has `id-token: write` + a named GitHub environment for the MCP Registry OIDC trust, (d) installs mcp-publisher and calls the OIDC-compatible publish subcommand with server.json.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `mcp-publisher` OIDC authentication protocol for the `mcp_registry` CI job: name the subcommand/flag (e.g., `login github-oidc` or an env-var token path), the required GitHub Actions permissions, and the mcp-publisher version that ships this capability.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `mcp-publisher` supports a GitHub Actions OIDC authentication path ('no interactive login'), but every codebase reference to mcp-publisher documents only `mcp-publisher login github` (browser/device-flow). No OIDC subcommand, flag, or environment-variable protocol for mcp-publisher is defined anywhere in the repo, making the processing protocol for the mcp_registry job's auth step entirely unspecified.
findings.location: Plan § 'What' — 'mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319)'; docs/releasing.md §4 'Update the MCP Registry' (lines 133-134)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the fallback for `mcp_registry` job failure: specify whether the job is re-triggerable (idempotent), whether manual `mcp-publisher publish` is the fallback, and how to confirm the registry state before re-running.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no fallback or recovery decision rule for the case where the `mcp_registry` job fails after PyPI and the GitHub Release have already succeeded. The only recovery guidance in the plan/docs is for the `github_release` job (idempotency, `gh release edit/delete`) and for PyPI (yank + version bump). The MCP Registry partial-failure path is unaddressed.
findings.location: Plan § 'Scope/steps'; docs/releasing.md § 'Fixing or retiring a bad GitHub Release' (lines 166-175)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Fixing a failed mcp_registry run' subsection to docs/releasing.md mirroring the existing github_release recovery section, noting the job's idempotency behavior and the manual fallback command.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a plan step (and AC) to update `docs/releasing.md` §4 and §'Accounts/prerequisites' to reflect the OIDC-automated MCP Registry path, replacing or annotating the `mcp-publisher login github` device-flow instructions.
findings.finding: `docs/releasing.md` §'Accounts/prerequisites' (line 27-28) still describes `mcp-publisher login github` (browser/device-flow) as the MCP Registry auth method, directly contradicting the plan's claim that OIDC-based non-interactive publishing is the method for v0.7.1. This instruction-locality gap means the runbook an executor follows says one thing while the plan claims another.
findings.location: docs/releasing.md lines 27-28 and lines 133-134 vs. Plan § 'What' and AC 'The MCP Registry 0.7.1 entry was published by the mcp_registry OIDC job (no interactive login)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
